bilateralTrust.note: wary engagement: dialogue channels exist, verification does not

compute.note: US holds the large majority of frontier training compute; 700/1000 maps that dominance with room to grow or lose it

energy.note: grid adequate today, tightening: IEA has datacentre demand at 415 TWh in 2024 rising to ~945 TWh by 2030 (base case)

talent.note: strong but contested US lead in frontier researchers per the AI Index workforce chapters; index mapping is design judgment

capital.note: record private AI investment through 2025-26 per the AI Index; index mapping is design judgment

publicTrust.note: public-opinion surveys show majority unease about AI; below midpoint by design

capability.note: frontier systems handle hour-scale tasks reliably; the fog zone starts far above

safetyInsight.note: interpretability real but young; technical safeguards immature per the 2025 IAISR second update

jobDisplacement.note: measurable early displacement in exposed occupations, not yet macro-visible

unrest.note: background political tension, no AI-specific movement yet

substitution.note: the US is not the export-controlled party; substitution is rules-inert for this seat

allocation.note: mirrors the current revealed preference of the ecosystem: capability-heavy

compute.note: export controls bite, but domestic accelerators plus DeepSeek-class efficiency keep real training capacity on the board

energy.note: grid buildout capacity is the seat's structural strength: generation added at a pace the US grid does not match

talent.note: research workforce at near-parity trends in the AI Index chapters; index mapping is design judgment

capital.note: state-directed funding deep but private-capital depth thinner than the US ecosystem

publicTrust.note: legitimacy, not polling: higher baseline, and the regime pays more when it slips (wargame observation: consistency advantage)

politicalCapital.note: top-down coordination moves faster: nationalization-style moves are cheaper and more available

capability.note: months behind the frontier, not years: DeepSeek-class efficiency closed much of the gap

safetyInsight.note: safety-institute capacity thinner; interpretability work younger

jobDisplacement.note: lower measured exposure in the IMF advanced-vs-emerging split at the start window

unrest.note: tighter information environment reads lower unrest at start; the ceiling is the same

substitution.note: domestic chip substitution underway (Huawei/SMIC), progress real but constrained

allocation.note: state-led capability push, thinner safety allocation at start
